Xinying Hao.review_text: Truly a wonderful professor. Professor Hao is so dedicated to her students and makes marketing analytics a breeze.I'd take her class again in a heartbeat.

Professor Hao is one of the best professors I've ever had!! She gave very inspirational lectures, and is very knowledgable and patient. This was my first time learning about most of the concepts in this class. I really enjoyed the class, even during this crazy time due to the pandemic.

This class was kind of a letdown because I did not learn much. This professor requires you to write down "contribution notes" for each class for your participation grade, which makes you focus on that instead of the actual lecture. The guidelines for this course were very confusing and this professor seemed confused as well, however, she is nice!

Sangeetha Venkataramani.review_text: Sangeetha is a great teacher. I struggled to learn BNAN in the huge lecture halls, but this analytics class was smaller, and Sangeetha won't move forward until everyone is solid. It was easy to ask for help, and this semester I gained way more excel skills. The course schedule isn't very accurate though, and you have to participate for a grade.
Sangeetha is super great! I loved her class and she always gave examples of practical applications for what we were learning.

She is an amazing professor! You definitely have to go to class 95% of the semester to grasp the content, because some of it is definitely advanced excel. However, this is a class that you genuinely WANT to go to because she is always such a positive, engaging, supportive teacher.

Amazing professor, I was scared of excel and felt like I knew nothing even though Eller has 3 Excel prereqs, she is the only teacher where things stuck. She connects everything to real business applications and is always willing to answer questions. You have to show up to class to be successful. I hope to have her again in the future!
Didn't expect to like this course, but I actually do like it very much. Very useful course. Even though it is way early at 8, still definitely worth it.
Very knowledgeable. I fell in love with analytics as a result of this course. I specifically asked for her for the MR course next semester.

Overall is a great professor who enjoys teaching her students and is great at it. I learned a lot in her class and she constantly asked if we had questions to make sure everyone was keeping up with the material. Attendance and participation are important in this class to succeed.
Not bad. Sangeetha made the course easy. She seems a bit strict, but is actually very sweet.

Sangeetha is one of the best professors I have had at UA. You definitely have to work for your grades and it's not an easy A but you learn SO much. Her lectures are so engaging. If you have the opportunity to take any class with her as a professor I would HIGHLY recommend it. She slays.
One of the best professors I have had.she is a bit fast through the subjects. Seems intimidating but she genuinely is concerned about all her students.

If you don't rate this professor well then you're a bad student honestly. I suck at Excel. I missed more classes than I should have. But I watched her lectures and asked my classmates for help. Exams are easy if you spend a few hours studying and she grades everything pretty easily. She's a sweetheart and is enthusiastic. Would 100% take again.

Sangeetha is an amazing professor who really cares about her students! As long as you explain to her your circumstances she is very understanding and will always want what's best for you. The course is excel heavy so as long as you pay attention and do the work you'll be fine. Overall, she's a great professor who enjoys teaching this subject!

Sangeetha is a very straight forward lecturer. All of the content on exams and homework is clearly gone over in class. There are not too many assignments, just enough to learn the topics. I enjoy her class and find that as long as you're paying attention to her, you will get good grades.

Sangeetha is a great professor and her class is very useful. If you zone out during class and fall behind on the lesson, it can be difficult to catch back up. Thankfully she posts recorded lectures online. Tests are about what you'd expect, and homework is easy if you know the content. Not the most exciting class, but very practical.

Sangeetha is by far one of my favorite professors in Eller. She cares about all her students and wants everyone to succeed. Excel is tough to learn, but she made it easy to understand. The homework is very similar to what you do in class, so it's very doable. She is always willing to help and is an amazing person all around!

Sangeetha is by far my favorite professor. Excel is just tough to learn, but Sangeetha does a great job teaching it. She really cares about her students and their mental health. I requested to take MKTG 450 with her because she is just the best.

Sangeetha gets a bad rep but is amazing. Students struggle with this class because it's all excel, but she explains everything thoroughly. The worst part of this class is the students. If you put in the effort you will get an A. The only criticism I have is that she's a bit disorganized, but she was also teaching 5 classes, so it's not her fault.

Im not sure why Sangeetha has such a low rating. Shes the only professor I had in my first semester Eller that cares. She gave us weeks off when we were stressed to take self care time. She also makes excel super easy to understand. She really cares for her students and her class is one of my favorites. I requested her next semester too.

Compared to other professors, Sangeetha is the most unorganized, incompetent professor. Makes mistakes in formulas and students have to correct her. Unhelpful, dismissive, and cold in office hrs. A huge red flag that she often finishes grading at 2-4AM! Once, she waited until midnight-2AM the night of the midterm to submit assignment grades!

Sangeetha is my absolute favorite professor at the U of A! She truly cares about her students understanding the material, but more importantly she cares about her students outside of the classroom. Also, she does not give trick questions on exams. Everything she tests you on is from the lectures.

Unorganized, uncaring, hostile. Our Final was on Wednesday, but she only posted the study guide a few days before. Screams and yells, sending passive aggressive emails in all caps, like REMEMBER TO SEND PARTICIPATION TRACKERS! LATE SUBMISSIONS WILL *NOT* BE ACCEPTED! Also, her feedback on assignments is unhelpful, negative, & passive aggressive.

In office hours, I tried to talk about how I dont have anyone to study with, but then she YELLED at me, IGNORED my point, and went on a tangent! Bruh, she is the one who said she was happy to see teams study together on Discord at the beginning of the year. She is DISRESPECTFUL, rude, inconsiderate, and doesnt listen!

Condescending and puts down students. Starts half-heartedly running the class as the semester ends by posting last minute study guides and announcements. Becomes careless and lazy, as seen in her late arrivals, unhelpful advice, and low effort organization of class structure. Makes All my other professors are more organized than her.

Inattentive - Makes many mistakes, which I have to remind her multiple times but she ignores and doesn't listen. Unhelpful feedback results in lower grades. Sometimes asks invasive questions. As the semester ends, starts slacking and becomes more unorganized and shows up late. Very unorganized - posts announcements and study guides last minute.

I loved her! She cares a lot and offered sooooo soooo many opportunities for you to receive a better grade on assignments and tests. Her slides were really helpful for all the exams and she did an awesome job at making sure the class all understood.

Roberta Schultz.review_text: Roberta is so nice and funny! she really cares about her students. She goes over homework and gives study guides which is really nice. I wasn't familiar with the sass software we have to use, but she explains it in a very clear manner. I averaged high Bs on all her exams, and I am hoping for an A in the class.

She goes over homework in class, which you should definitely attend, because her instructions may as well be written in Hindi. Same thing on tests. She's decent at teaching the material, and I already had a firm grip on stats from previous courses, but I literally have no idea what she's asking me on homework, tests, and in class.

I liked her but I understood the content of the class. Class is not hard compared to the other stats courses. Go to class, take notes you can easily get an A in the class. She usually tells you what is on the test in lecture and will give feedback on hw before it's due. Can be confusing with instructions, ask for clarification, she will give it.
Great professor, however the lectures can tend to be dull, so it is important to pay attention. She lays out all criteria for assignments and eliminates all ambiguity
